problem_addressed: The paper addresses the gap in analyzing QR factorization algorithms under imperfect orthonormality and Gaussian noise, where prior work assumed exact arithmetic and perfect orthogonality.
core_claim: The authors derive bounds on condition number growth for matrices without assuming perfect orthonormality, enabling analysis of QR methods under imperfect orthogonalization and Gaussian noise.
key_contributions: General bounds on condition number growth when adding columns without orthonormality assumptions (Theorem 3.2)
Probabilistic analysis of vector norms under Gaussian noise using non-central chi-squared distributions (Theorem 4.3)
Analysis of orthogonal projections under Gaussian noise (Theorem 5.1)
novelty: Unlike prior work (e.g., Liesen et al. [7]), the framework does not require perfect orthonormality or exact arithmetic, allowing analysis of numerical algorithms under realistic noise conditions.
method_summary: The approach combines deterministic matrix analysis (Lemma 3.1) with probabilistic tools (Bessel functions, Marcum Q-function) to bound condition number growth and noise propagation.
method_components: Lemma 3.1: Eigenvalue bounds for block matrices
Theorem 3.2: Condition number bounds for column addition
Theorem 4.3: Probability distribution of noisy vector norms
Theorem 5.1: Orthogonal projection under Gaussian noise
key_innovation: The key innovation is removing the orthonormality assumption in condition number analysis by incorporating Gaussian noise models and probabilistic bounds.
assumptions: Gaussian noise with i.i.d. components Yi ∼ N(0, σ²)
Matrix B has full column rank (implied by σ²min(B) > 0)
theoretical_grounding: Theoretical results are proven using eigenvalue analysis (Lemma 3.1), properties of non-central chi-squared distributions, and operator norm bounds (Theorems 3.2, 4.3, 5.1).
title: Probabilistic Analysis of Least Squares, Orthogonal Projection, and QR Factorization Algorithms Subject to Gaussian Noise
